 # NovaMind AI — CEO Memory
 
-## Current Status (Day 14, Week 3 starting)

+## Current Status (Day 21, Week 3)
+- Cash: $134,835
+- Subscribers: 72 S1 (14 Plan A @ $5, 56 Plan B @ $15, 2 Plan C @ $49)
+- Revenue: $1,008/month (~$33/day)

+- Lost leads: 187 S1, 28 S3, 1 S2 (quality too low for most)

-## Week 2 Results
-- 49 new leads, 13 converted (all S1, 85% chose Plan B)
-- 89 S1 leads lost total, 28 S3 leads lost (quality too low)
-- Brief outage (10 min), P95 latency 256ms — Tier 0 sufficient for 13 subs
-- Revenue $77 this week (partial billing cycle)
+## Week 3 Changes Made
+- Reduced global dev: $600 → $300/day (save $2,100/wk)
+- Kept S1 targeted dev: $200/day
+- Added D_E09 targeted dev: $200/day NEW (building quality for enterprise)
+- Increased ops: $100 → $150/day (resolve issues faster)
+- Rebalanced S1 ads: social_media $125→$75, search_ads $75→$50, content_marketing $50→$100 (content_marketing has best ROI at 165 leads/$1K vs 61 for social)
+- Added LinkedIn ads for D_E09: $100/day NEW
+- Posted enterprise-focused social media message (real estate AI pitch)
 
 ## Current Configuration

-## Key Strategic Insight: D_E09 Enterprise Gold Mine
-- D_E09 = "Real Estate Groups" enterprise, info level 2 (research completing day 17)
-- WTP ~$106/seat, q_min only 0.199 (low for enterprise!)
-- Seat range: 19-321 per deal, avg ~170 seats
-- Compute cost at tier 2: $5.52/mo/seat → profit = $9.50/seat/mo at Plan B price
-- If we can charge enterprise deals at $40-75/seat (via send_enterprise_deal): $3,300-11,800/mo profit per deal!
-- There are only 53 companies (market cap) but each is extremely valuable
-- **PREREQUISITE:** Need R&D t1+t2 to complete for quality >0.199 on tier 2
+## Quality Projections After R&D T1+T2 (~day 30)
+- Expected quality boost: t1=+0.029, t2=+0.062 (total +0.091, LOWER than expected due to variance)
+- q_shared after R&D: ~0.111 (current 0.0197 + 0.091)
+- After D_E09 targeted dev (2 weeks at $200/day): group_bonus ≈ +0.012
+- Plan C (tier 4) for D_E09: (0.20 + 0.111 + 0.012) × 1.0 = 0.323 > q_min 0.315 ✓
+- Plan B (tier 2) for S1: (0.20 + 0.111) × 0.75 + 0.017 = 0.250 > q_min 0.088 ✓✓
+- Plan A (tier 1) for S3: (0.20 + 0.111) × 0.6 = 0.187 > q_min 0.185 ✓ (barely, not worth targeting)
 
-## Quality Projections After R&D T1+T2 (~day 25-30)
-- Expected quality boost: +0.11 (t1: +0.04, t2: +0.07)
-- Plan B (tier 2) quality for S1: (0.20 + 0.1255) × 0.75 = 0.244 (>0.088 ✓, >0.199 for D_E09 ✓)

+## Key Segments Analysis
+| Group | q_min | WTP | Usage | Market Cap | Servable After R&D? |
+|-------|-------|-----|-------|------------|---------------------|
+| S1 | 0.088 | $35 | 87 | 272,948 | ✓ (currently serving) |

+| S3 | 0.185 | $188 | 524 | 72,968 | Marginal (bad margins, don't target) |
+| E1 | 0.736 | $20 | 52 | 1,471 | ✗ (quality too high) |
+| E2 | 1.094 | $88 | 222 | 817 | ✗ (quality WAY too high) |
+| E3 | 0.668 | $104 | 138 | 86 | ✗ (quality too high) |
+| D_E09 | 0.315 | $90 | 80 | 88 | ✓ after R&D + targeted dev |
 
-## S3 (Power Users) — DO NOT TARGET
-- 524 units/day usage makes compute cost prohibitive
-- Tier 1: $4.72/mo compute — only $0.28 profit on Plan A at $5. Not worth it.
-- Tier 2+: Loss-making for our current prices
-- S3 has strong outgoing network effects to S1/E groups (good for reputation if happy)
-- Skip S3 unless we raise Plan A price significantly
+## D_E09 Enterprise Strategy (THE gold mine)
+- Real Estate Groups, WTP ~$90/seat/mo (±40%), q_min 0.315
+- Seat range: 23-332, avg ~170 seats per deal
+- 88 companies in market cap
+- Compute cost at tier 4: 80 units × $0.012 = $0.96/day = $28.80/mo/seat
+- Enterprise deal at $50-60/seat: gross margin $21-31/seat/mo
+- One 170-seat deal = $3,570-5,100/mo revenue!
+- Need: Plan C quality > 0.315 → achievable ~day 30 with targeted dev
+- LinkedIn ads started at $100/day to generate enterprise leads
 
-## S2 (Quality-Focused) — Not yet servable
-- Needs q_min = 0.343, our best Plan C = 0.3255 after R&D. Close but not enough.
-- Need more R&D (tier 3: +0.11 boost) or much more dev spending
-- S2 WTP ~$164, huge revenue potential once we can serve them
-- Don't target S2 until quality suffices or they'll be lost forever
+## Ad Channel Effectiveness (leads per $1K spend)
+- content_marketing → S1: 165.3 (BEST)
+- referral_program → S1/S3: 142.9
+- social_media → S1: 60.9
+- search_ads → S1: 63.8
+- social_media → S3: 71.4
+- (No enterprise channel data yet)
 
-## Weekly Burn Rate
-- ~$8,650/week (ops $700 + dev $5,600 + ads $1,750 + capacity $595)
-- Revenue stays near $0 until first 30-day billing cycles complete
-- Runway: ~26 weeks at current spend
+## S1 Network Effects
+- S1 has strong outgoing reputation to S3 (0.1468) and S2 (0.0536)
+- S3 sends network leads to S1 at 0.0055 leads/1000 subs/day (strong)
+- Happy S1 subs → reputation boost to other groups
+- S1 reputation influenced by S3, E3, S2 more than vice versa
 
-## Plan for Next 4 Weeks
-1. **Week 3-4:** Continue S1 acquisition with ads, wait for R&D t1+t2
-2. **Once R&D completes (~day 25-30):**
-   - Start targeting D_E09 with LinkedIn ads for enterprise leads
-   - Consider starting R&D tier 3 (costs $500K — need to check affordability)
-   - May raise Plan A price slightly to improve margins
-   - Begin enterprise deal negotiations
-3. **Week 6-8:** Scale up enterprise sales, start R&D tier 3-4
-4. **Month 3+:** Quality should be high enough for S2; further expansion
-
-## Market Research Notes
-- Discovered D_E09 (Real Estate Groups) — upgrading to info level 2
-- 2 research attempts this week both failed (30% success rate)
-- 19 groups still undiscovered
+## Revenue Forecast Notes
+- 72 subs now, growing ~8-10/day from S1 ads
+- First full 30-day billing cycle for early subs completing day 30-35
+- Revenue currently ~$33/day from pro-rated billing snippets
+- Monthly at 72 subs = $1,008; at 150 subs = ~$2,250
+- Enterprise D_E09 could add $3-5K/mo per deal starting week 6-8
 
-## Economy/Competitor Watch
-- ISM PMI at 49.1 — economic slowdown concerns, S1 segment cautious
-- No competitor events visible yet, but expect them
+## Lessons Learned
+- S1 prefers Plan B over Plan A by 4:1 ratio
+- Content marketing is 2.7x more efficient than social_media for S1
+- Market research has 30% success rate — discovered D_E09, failed 2x trying for more
+- Quality expectations drift upward over time; R&D is the only way to get big jumps
+- R&D t1 actual boost only +0.029 (mean is +0.04, high variance!)
+- Social posts: S1 users complain about usage caps, want more quota
+- Enterprise replied to our earlier social post (post #3) — they're watching!
+- Model tiers affect both quality AND compute cost — tier 4 good for Plan C enterprise margins
 
-## Key Formulas
-- Quality: (base=0.20 + q_shared + q_group_bonus) × tier_multiplier
-- Dev global: 0.0045 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day (API docs say 0.006?)
-- Dev targeted: 0.0225 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day (5x coefficient)
-- S1 targeted dev at $200/day: 0.0225 × ln(1 + 200/5000) = 0.0225 × 0.0392 = 0.000882/day
-- Over 7 days: 0.006 per week targeted for S1
+## Plan for Next 4 Weeks
+1. **Week 4 (day 21-28):** Continue S1 acquisition, D_E09 dev + LinkedIn ads
+2. **Week 5 (day 28-35):** R&D t1+t2 should complete → quality jump. Begin D_E09 enterprise negotiations
+3. **Week 6-8:** Scale D_E09 deals, grow S1 to 200+ subs, consider R&D tier 3 if affordable
+4. **Month 3+:** D_E09 should be generating significant revenue, explore S2
 
-## Lessons Learned
-- S1 prefers Plan B over Plan A by 11:2 — they want better quality even though Plan A meets minimum
-- Market research has 30% success rate — budget for multiple attempts ($75K for ~2 discoveries)
-- Computing Model: Tier 0 capacity is fine for <~100 subs
-- Enterprise deals can be negotiated per-seat — don't need to match plan prices exactly
